_comment: Canonical source roster for the Daily Tech Briefing (general technology: big tech & business, devices & consumer, software & platforms, science & emerging tech). The 6 AM scheduled task reads this file to build the Tech section and APPENDS newly vetted sources here over time. Categories: bigtech | devices | software | science. Tiers: core | strong | newsletter. Do not remove the seeded core sources without reason. This is a sibling roster to the Cyber and AI briefings; it deliberately avoids pure cybersecurity and pure AI-model coverage.

discoveryRules.criteria: Add only sources that are (1) reputable and editorially credible, (2) primarily original reporting/analysis rather than aggregation, (3) actively publishing in 2026, (4) clearly relevant to general technology news (big tech & business, devices & consumer products, software & platforms, or science & emerging tech) rather than narrow cybersecurity or pure AI-model news already covered by sibling briefings, and (5) not already present (match by domain). Prefer primary sources and outlets that break original stories. When unsure, log as a candidate instead of adding.

sources.name: The Verge
sources.note: High-volume, well-reported coverage across big tech, policy, devices, and platforms.

sources.name: TechCrunch
sources.note: Startups, funding, M&A, and big-tech business news; strong original deal reporting.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.note: Technically rigorous reporting across computing, science, policy, and consumer tech.

sources.name: Bloomberg Technology
sources.note: Original business/markets reporting on big tech, earnings, deals, and supply chains.

sources.name: Reuters Technology
sources.note: Wire-service breaking news on deals, earnings, regulation, and global tech.

sources.name: CNBC Technology
sources.note: Markets-focused tech and business coverage; earnings, IPOs, and executive moves.

sources.name: The Information
sources.note: Subscription outlet known for scoops on big-tech strategy, deals, and internals.

sources.name: Engadget
sources.note: Consumer electronics, gadgets, and hands-on device coverage with daily news.

sources.name: The Gadgeteer
sources.note: Long-running independent gadget reviews and new-product roundups.

sources.name: 9to5Mac
sources.note: Primary-source Apple ecosystem reporting, devices, and software releases.

sources.name: Tom's Hardware
sources.note: Deep PC hardware, chips, and component benchmarking and news.

sources.name: The Register
sources.note: Enterprise IT, cloud, and software/platform news with original reporting.

sources.name: ZDNet
sources.note: Business technology, software platforms, and enterprise product coverage.

sources.name: Ars Technica Science
sources.note: Rigorous coverage of space, physics, and emerging science with technical depth.

sources.name: Space.com
sources.note: Spaceflight, astronomy, and space-industry news; strong launch/mission coverage.

sources.name: Stratechery (Ben Thompson)
sources.note: Influential analysis newsletter on tech strategy, business models, and platform economics.

sources.name: Crunchbase News
sources.note: Data-driven original reporting on startups, venture funding, M&A, and unicorn trends.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's robotics venture-funding story ($23B+ in 2026). Reputable, primarily original data-driven reporting on private markets and startup funding, actively publishing throughout 2026, clearly general-tech business rather than narrow cyber or pure AI-model news. New domain (news.crunchbase.com) not previously in roster.

sources.name: 404 Media
sources.note: Journalist-owned independent outlet doing original, investigative reporting on internet platforms, right-to-repair, online communities, and consumer-tech accountability.
sources.rationale: Surfaced via source-discovery search for original tech journalism. Reputable (EFF Pioneer Award), reporter-owned, primarily original/investigative rather than aggregation, actively publishing in 2026, and fills a platforms/internet-culture/right-to-repair gap distinct from narrow cyber or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (404media.co) not previously in roster.

sources.name: Rest of World
sources.note: Nonprofit publication with on-the-ground original reporting on technology's impact across emerging markets beyond the Western tech bubble.
sources.rationale: Surfaced via source-discovery search for original tech journalism. Reputable nonprofit, primarily original on-the-ground reporting (not aggregation), actively publishing in 2026, and broadens coverage of global big-tech/business and devices beyond US-centric outlets already in the roster. New domain (restofworld.org) not previously in roster.

sources.name: SpaceNews
sources.note: Independent, award-winning space-industry publication (since 1989) covering launches, spacecraft, satellite, and space-business news with original reporting.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's SpaceX Starfall reentry-capsule demo story. Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible (independent space journalism since 1989, 500k+ professional audience), primarily original reporting/analysis rather than aggregation, actively publishing in 2026, clearly relevant to science & emerging tech (space industry) and distinct from narrow cyber or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (spacenews.com) not previously in roster; complements Space.com and Ars Technica Science with deeper space-business and launch coverage.

sources.name: SiliconANGLE
sources.note: Enterprise-tech and platforms news site with original reporting and analysis on cloud, AI infrastructure, chips, and software business.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's Meituan LongCat-2.0 open-source / domestic-chip story. Reputable and editorially credible, primarily original reporting/analysis rather than aggregation, actively publishing throughout 2026, clearly relevant to general software/platforms and big-tech business rather than narrow cyber or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (siliconangle.com) not previously in roster.

sources.name: Tom's Guide
sources.note: Consumer-facing tech site with original hands-on testing, reviews, and buying guidance across phones, wearables, audio, and smart home.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's Samsung Galaxy Unpacked (July 22) preview. Reputable, primarily original hands-on reviews/testing rather than aggregation, actively publishing in 2026, and fills a consumer-devices/reviews niche distinct from sibling Tom's Hardware's components focus. New domain (tomsguide.com) not previously in roster (distinct from tomshardware.com).

sources.name: IEEE Spectrum
sources.note: Flagship publication of IEEE with original, technically authoritative journalism on computing, robotics, semiconductors, energy, and emerging engineering.
sources.rationale: Surfaced via a source-discovery search for reputable original technology/engineering journalism. Highly credible (IEEE, the world's largest technical professional org), primarily original reporting and analysis rather than aggregation, actively publishing in 2026, and broadens science & emerging-tech coverage with engineering depth distinct from narrow cyber or pure AI-model news. New domain (spectrum.ieee.org) not previously in roster.

sources.name: Spaceflight Now
sources.note: Long-running independent space-news outlet providing original launch coverage, mission reporting, and live launch broadcasts from Cape Canaveral and beyond.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's SpaceX Starlink launch (24 satellites from Vandenberg, 632nd booster recovery). Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible (leading online space-news source with original on-the-pad reporting and 24/7 live coverage), primarily original reporting rather than aggregation, actively publishing throughout 2026, clearly relevant to science & emerging tech (spaceflight) and distinct from narrow cyber or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (spaceflightnow.com) not previously in roster; complements Space.com, SpaceNews, and Ars Technica Science with granular launch-by-launch coverage.

sources.name: Electrek
sources.note: Original reporting on electric vehicles, energy, and green transportation tech, including primary-source quarterly delivery/production analysis for Tesla, Rivian, and other EV makers.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's Tesla Q2 record-deliveries (480,126) and Rivian raised-guidance stories. Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible, primarily original reporting and analysis (not aggregation) on EVs, batteries, and clean-energy hardware, actively publishing throughout 2026, and clearly relevant to general devices/consumer and big-tech business rather than narrow cybersecurity or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (electrek.co) not previously in roster; fills an EV/energy-tech beat none of the existing sources cover directly.

sources.name: GeekWire
sources.note: Seattle-based national technology news site (since 2011) with extensive original reporting on Amazon, Microsoft, cloud, startups, space, and the broader tech-business ecosystem.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's Amazon Leo story (that the latest Atlas V launch gives Amazon enough satellites to begin commercial broadband service). Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible (national tech publication with Seattle DNA, profiled by Nieman Lab), primarily original reporting rather than aggregation, actively publishing throughout 2026, and clearly relevant to general big-tech/business, cloud, and space rather than narrow cybersecurity or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (geekwire.com) not previously in roster; strengthens Amazon/Microsoft and cloud/space-business coverage.

sources.name: The New Stack
sources.note: DevOps, open-source, and cloud-native news site with original reporting and analysis on Kubernetes, platform engineering, developer tooling, and AI infrastructure.
sources.rationale: Surfaced via a source-discovery search for reputable original enterprise/cloud journalism, and relevant to today's open-source-cloud thread (OpenStack Gazpacho workload migration, CNCF/Kubernetes adoption). Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible, primarily original reporting/analysis rather than aggregation, actively publishing throughout 2026, and clearly relevant to general software/platforms (cloud-native, DevOps, developer infrastructure) rather than narrow cybersecurity or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (thenewstack.io) not previously in roster; fills a developer/cloud-native/open-source beat distinct from The Register's and ZDNet's enterprise-IT focus.

sources.name: Windows Central
sources.note: Editorially independent Windows/Xbox enthusiast publication with a small dedicated staff producing original reporting, exclusives, and hands-on reviews rather than aggregation.
sources.rationale: Surfaced repeatedly as the original source behind Windows 11 feature-update coverage used in the briefing over the past week (including today's cycle). Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible (staff bios show original reporting standards, no pay-for-coverage policy), primarily original rather than aggregation, actively publishing throughout 2026, clearly relevant to general software/platforms rather than narrow cyber or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (windowscentral.com) not previously in roster despite being cited multiple times; promoting it now formalizes a source already relied upon.

sources.name: Game File (Stephen Totilo)
sources.note: Reader-supported gaming-industry newsletter from veteran reporter Stephen Totilo (ex-Kotaku editor-in-chief, ex-Axios), covering the business and culture of video games with original scoops and analysis.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's granular Xbox studio-divestment reporting (which studios, why, and what's next) that expanded on the Microsoft layoffs story. Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible (20-year veteran reporter, ~25,000 subscribers), primarily original reporting/scoops rather than aggregation, actively publishing in 2026, and clearly relevant to big-tech/business (gaming industry) rather than narrow cyber or pure AI-model coverage. New domain (gamefile.news) not previously in roster.

sources.name: The Block
sources.note: New York-based digital-assets news outlet (founded 2018) known for data-driven, business-focused original reporting on crypto markets, exchanges, fintech infrastructure, and regulation.
sources.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's Coinbase UK FCA investment-services authorization story. Passes discovery criteria: reputable and editorially credible (rigorous editorial standards, trusted by institutional/professional readers), primarily original reporting/analysis rather than aggregation, actively publishing throughout 2026, and relevant to general big-tech/business (the intersection of crypto exchanges, fintech, and traditional finance) rather than narrow cybersecurity or pure AI-model coverage. Distinguished from candidate TFTC by broader, business-grade editorial reach beyond bitcoin culture. New domain (theblock.co) not previously in roster.

candidates.name: FERC News & Rulemakings
candidates.note: Primary U.S. energy regulator; authoritative on data-center grid interconnection and large-load rules.
candidates.rationale: Authoritative primary source behind today's large-load interconnection story, increasingly relevant to AI-infrastructure/grid coverage. Parked as a candidate rather than added because it is a government docket/press portal rather than an editorial reporting outlet; revisit if grid/energy-tech stories recur.

candidates.name: EE Times
candidates.note: Long-running electronics-engineering trade publication with original semiconductor, chip-industry, and components reporting.
candidates.rationale: Surfaced via a source-discovery search for original hardware/semiconductor journalism, highly relevant given today's chip-heavy news (Micron, SK Hynix, the memory-shortage price hikes). Reputable and original rather than aggregation, but parked as a candidate this cycle because it did not directly originate a story used today and the daily add limit was reserved for SpaceNews; promote if semiconductor/components stories recur.

candidates.name: Semiconductor Engineering
candidates.note: Deep, engineer-authored coverage of chip design, verification, manufacturing, and semiconductor market dynamics.
candidates.rationale: Surfaced via source-discovery search for original hardware/semiconductor journalism. Reputable and original rather than aggregation, actively publishing in 2026, but parked as a candidate because its niche EDA/chip-design focus skews technical/trade and the daily add limit (3) was reached with SiliconANGLE, Tom's Guide, and IEEE Spectrum; promote if chip-design/manufacturing stories recur.

candidates.name: Dealroom
candidates.note: Venture/startup market-intelligence platform whose funding datasets are widely cited for VC, M&A, and sector-funding totals.
candidates.rationale: Authoritative primary data source behind today's robotics-funding figure ($55.8B raised in 2026, ~2x the prior record). Parked as a candidate rather than added because it is a data/analytics platform rather than an original editorial reporting outlet (its numbers are surfaced through outlets like CNBC and Crunchbase News already in the roster); promote if it begins originating standalone reporting used directly in the briefing.

candidates.name: The Quantum Insider
candidates.note: Dedicated quantum-technology news outlet covering quantum computing, sensing, and related research with original reporting and analysis.
candidates.rationale: Surfaced as the original source behind today's quantum arrow-of-time / measurement-energy-harvesting research (Physical Review X). Reputable and primarily original within its niche, actively publishing in 2026, but parked as a candidate rather than added because its coverage is narrowly quantum-focused (a specialist trade beat that only intermittently intersects the general-tech briefing) and the daily add slot was used for GeekWire; promote if quantum stories recur and originate here.

candidates.name: TFTC (Truth for the Commoner)
candidates.note: Bitcoin/economics/culture newsletter that occasionally covers technology and AI-supply-chain stories with original reporting.
candidates.rationale: Surfaced as the source of today's Apple-Pentagon-blacklisted-chipmakers story. Reviewed but not added: its core editorial identity and majority of coverage is bitcoin/crypto-focused rather than general technology, making it a narrower fit than the roster's discovery criteria intend; park as a candidate and revisit only if it originates further general-tech stories.

candidates.name: Phys.org
candidates.note: High-volume science and technology news site covering physics, materials, space, and emerging research.
candidates.rationale: Surfaced as the source used for today's machine-learning-guided superconductor-discovery story (SuperC consortium, YRu3B2/LuRu3B2). Reviewed but parked as a candidate rather than added because its model leans toward republishing and lightly editing institutional press releases rather than primarily original reporting, which does not clearly meet discovery criterion (2); revisit if it demonstrates more original science journalism or recurs as the best available source.
